# agennect-open

> Self-hosted A2A (Agent-to-Agent) registry with an MCP (Model Context
> Protocol) server directory. Single Node 20 container, SQLite-backed,
> vanilla HTML dashboard. The open-source core of Agennect.

Use this file to learn how to register agents, list MCP servers, and
record invocations. Every endpoint listed here is reachable at both
`/v1/<path>` (canonical) and `/<path>` (legacy alias).

## Auth model

- `read` scope: most GETs are public and need no auth.
- `write` scope: required for `POST/PUT/DELETE /agents` and `/mcp`.
  Tokens stamp `owner_user_id` on resources they create; they can only
  mutate their own.
- `admin` scope: required for `/admin/*` (tokens, users, audit,
  webhooks). Admins can mutate anything.
- Header: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. The dashboard exchanges a
  Firebase ID token for a session token via `POST /auth/login`.

## Key endpoints

### Registering an agent (write or admin)

POST /v1/agents
```json
{
  "name": "DataOracle",
  "description": "Specialized agent for exploratory data analysis (min 20 chars)",
  "provider": "Agennect",
  "protocols": ["A2A"],
  "endpoint_url": "https://your.agent/tasks",
  "hosting": "referenced",
  "auth_type": "none",
  "capabilities": ["EDA", "SQL"]
}
```

Response 201:
```json
{ "id": "dataoracle-ab", "status": "created", "agent_card_url": "..." }
```

### Discovery

- `GET /v1/agents` — list, filters: `status`, `protocol`, `hosting`,
  `mine` (when authed), `page`, `limit`.
- `GET /v1/agents/search?q=<text>` — semantic search via sqlite-vec.
- `GET /v1/agents/{id}` — full agent record.
- `GET /v1/agents/{id}/.well-known/agent.json` — A2A Agent Card.
- `GET /v1/mcp` — list MCP servers.

### Reporting invocations (Mode B — recommended)

POST /v1/agents/{id}/report
```json
{
  "latency_ms": 340,
  "status": "success",
  "request_size": 512,
  "response_size": 1024
}
```

Response 200: `{ "ok": true, "invocation_id": "..." }`.

There are JS and Python SDKs at `src/sdk/reporter.{js,py}` that wrap
this in two lines.

### Invoking via proxy (Mode A — opt-in)

Per-agent flag. Enable: `PUT /v1/agents/{id} { "proxy_enabled": true }`.
Then `POST /v1/agents/{id}/tasks` with an A2A Task body. The registry
forwards to the agent's `endpoint_url`, logs latency, and returns the
response with an `_agennect` metadata block.

### Metrics

- `GET /v1/metrics` — global KPIs (p50/p95 latency, mode breakdown,
  top agents, agents up/down).
- `GET /v1/metrics/agents/{id}` — per-agent (p50/p95/p99 + hourly_last_24h).
- `GET /v1/metrics/timeseries?hours=24&agent_id=...` — hourly buckets.

### Webhooks (admin)

- `POST /v1/admin/webhooks { name, url, events }` — events are dot-separated
  (`agent.create`, `mcp.update`, `webhook.test`, `user.login`, ...) or `*`.
- Delivery: signed POST with header
  `X-Agennect-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hmac>`.
  Signing key is `sha256(secret)`; verify with HMAC-SHA256.
- `POST /v1/admin/webhooks/{id}/test` — fire a synthetic event for
  receiver verification.

## Schemas

The full OpenAPI 3.1 spec is hand-maintained and served live:

- YAML — `GET /openapi.yaml`
- JSON — `GET /openapi.json`

## Errors

All errors are JSON: `{ "error": "<message>" }`.

| Status | Meaning                                                         |
|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| 400    | Validation failed (zod error message in `error`)                |
| 401    | Missing or invalid token                                        |
| 403    | Token scope insufficient OR not the owner                       |
| 404    | Resource not found                                              |
| 422    | Proxy mode disabled (with a `hint` field)                       |
| 502    | Proxy upstream returned non-2xx                                 |
| 504    | Proxy upstream timed out                                        |

## Constraints when writing client code

- All requests SHOULD pass `X-Caller-ID: <name>` so the registry can
  attribute calls in metrics and the audit log.
- For Mode A proxy, forward auth to the upstream agent via
  `X-Agent-Auth: <value>` — the registry never persists it.
- Webhook receivers MUST verify the signature in constant time.
- Token plaintexts are returned exactly once on creation. Lose it and
  you have to mint a new one.
